build_summary: This was an underdeveloped Silent frontload deck that tried to win short fights with direct attack damage, then improved consistency with double Acrobatics and a strong Pinpoint hit. It never found a real scaling or defensive engine, so it remained heavily reliant on starter cards and got outlasted by Lagavulin Matriarch.
damage_engine: Mostly direct attack damage, with Pinpoint as the clearest final-deck payoff and earlier combats also leaning on Strangle, Poisoned Stab, Slice, and Dash for chip and burst; no durable scaling package was assembled.
defense_engine: Primarily basic Defends and Survivor, with some earlier efficiency from Dash; by the final deck, defense was still largely starter-card blocking plus Weak from Neutralize+.
cycle_engine: Acrobatics + Acrobatics+ for inferred draw/discard cycling in a small 15-card deck.
energy_engine: base 3 energy
weak_points: The deck was worst at long elite fights because it lacked both scaling damage and a reliable non-basic block engine.

key_cards.insight: It was the most important high-impact payoff left in the final deck, contributing 50 traced damage and serving as the closest thing to a burst finisher.
Played 10 times and repeatedly applied Weak, helping the deck survive despite having mostly basic block cards.
One of the few cards that improved consistency, helping a compact deck find Pinpoint, Survivor, or Neutralize more often.

build_summary: This was a starter-heavy Silent deck that tried to win with small poison chip and safe play patterns, using draw/retention to line up blocks and key attacks. It had some useful support pieces, but most real output still came from basic Strikes and Defends rather than a fully developed poison or block engine.
damage_engine: Backstab opener and repeated Strikes did most traceable damage, with Poisoned Stab and Deadly Poison adding modest poison-based scaling.
defense_engine: Mostly Defend + Survivor + Backflip, with Well-Laid Plans+ helping hold the right cards; block was fundamentally starter-card based.
cycle_engine: Backflip + Acrobatics/Acrobatics+ (inferred), plus Well-Laid Plans+ retention.
energy_engine: Base 3 energy; only negligible incidental gain from Neutralize+/generated Shiv traces, with no dedicated energy card.
weak_points: It was worst at long or high-pressure fights because the deck never found enough focused scaling, so it leaned too hard on basic attacks and blocks.

key_cards.insight: Its 110 traced damage across 11 exhausts made it the cleanest proactive damage card in the deck, especially for opening turns.
It supplied both direct damage and poison, making it one of the few non-starter attacks that consistently advanced the deck's plan.
This was the deck's clearest scaling damage piece, but the overall poison package was too small for it to carry fights by itself.
75 traced block plus card draw made it one of the deck's best consistency tools and an important upgrade over basic defense.
Retention supported a slow, reactive game plan by preserving scarce payoff cards and key defensive turns.
Leading the damage chart at 329 shows the deck never replaced its starter offense with a stronger dedicated engine.

build_summary: This was a thin Silent deck that mainly tried to survive with Dexterity-scaled block and retain key cards with Well-Laid Plans while wearing enemies down with Dagger Spray, Cloak and Dagger shivs, and other chip attacks. It had a light poison backup plan, but too much of the actual damage still came from basic attacks, so it never became a clean finisher deck.
damage_engine: Mostly Dagger Spray+ and Cloak and Dagger shivs/basic attacks, with Deadly Poison and Bouncing Flask+ as secondary poison support.
defense_engine: Footwork/Footwork+ scaling on Defends, Cloak and Dagger, Survivor, Backflip+, and Dodge and Roll.
cycle_engine: Prepared+ and Backflip+ for light cycling, with Well-Laid Plans retention support (inferred).
energy_engine: none observed; base 3 energy
weak_points: It was worst at closing tougher fights, because the deck defended reasonably well but lacked strong scaling damage or energy acceleration and still relied heavily on Strikes/chip damage.

key_cards.insight: Dexterity scaling was the clearest defensive identity of the run, repeatedly boosting otherwise basic block cards into a workable defense plan.
It was the single biggest traceable damage source at 306, showing that multi-hit chip/AoE was the deck's most reliable way to end fights.
It contributed 284 traceable block while also generating shivs, making it one of the few cards that advanced both offense and defense.

Its offense was split between shivs, Dagger Spray chip, and a small poison package, so it could stabilize fights but struggled to end tougher ones quickly.
damage_engine: Hybrid damage from Shiv generation and Dagger Spray chip, supplemented by Deadly Poison and Bouncing Flask poison stacking; Pinpoint+ also provided a notable burst source during the run.
defense_engine: Double Footwork+ plus Backflip/Backflip+, Cloak and Dagger, Deflect/Deflect+, Dodge and Roll, and basic blocks.

energy_engine: Only light traceable card-based energy, mainly occasional +1 from Pinpoint+ and Dagger Spray+; otherwise this looked close to base-energy play.
weak_points: It was weakest at closing long boss fights, because damage scaling was slow and split across multiple sub-plans while the 30-card deck remained energy-hungry and inconsistent.

build_summary: This was a large Silent control deck that tried to survive with Footwork-enhanced skills, Backflip-based blocking, and Apparition turns while cycling through a small discard package. It aimed to win over time through poison, with Pinpoint+ acting as the biggest burst finisher when drawn.
damage_engine: Pinpoint+ provided the largest traceable burst, backed by Poison from Deadly Poison and Bouncing Flask, with Noxious Fumes/Outbreak as supporting slow damage pieces.
defense_engine: Footwork+ scaling on Backflip, Deflect, Cloak and Dagger, Survivor, and Dodge and Roll, plus Apparition+ for emergency Intangible turns.
cycle_engine: Backflip + Prepared+ + Calculated Gamble+ with Reflex/Tactician discard payoffs (inferred).
energy_engine: No clear traceable energy generation was observed; mostly base energy, with possible occasional Tactician spikes if discard outlets lined up.
weak_points: Its biggest problem was consistency: at 37 cards, the poison plan, discard package, and burst tools were all diluted, so the deck could stall but not reliably close fights.

key_cards.insight: Only 3 recorded plays produced 66 traceable damage, making it the deck's single biggest burst source by a wide margin.
Two copies gave the many cheap block skills a coherent defensive shell and likely amplified most of the listed block sources.

coherence_analysis: The Footwork/Apparition/Backflip shell gave the deck a real control identity, but the final list was split between poison, discard payoffs, and scattered burst cards, leaving it noticeably unfocused and bloated.

It could survive normal fights with basics plus Piercing Wail, but it never fully committed to either a strong poison stall plan or a strong burst damage plan.

cycle_engine: Dagger Throw + Acrobatics+ + Prepared+ + Backflip+ (inferred draw/discard package).

build_summary: This looked like a bulky Silent deck trying to win with Accuracy-backed Shiv damage and a few strong attack bursts like Pounce+, while staying alive through Apparitions, Footwork, and temporary mitigation. weak_points: Its biggest problem was consistency: the 42-card list was bloated with basics and off-plan filler, so it often failed to line up Shiv pressure, Apparitions, and enough scaling in time.

It was the second-largest traceable damage source and also granted Free Skill, making it one of the few cards that improved both tempo and burst.
It contributed traceable block while also fitting the Shiv plan, making it one of the cleaner bridge cards between offense and defense.

This was the deck's main long-term block scaling and helped make the many mediocre block cards more serviceable.

build_summary: This was a light Shiv tempo deck that tried to win by generating lots of Shivs and converting them into burst damage with observed Accuracy+/Phantom Blades scaling, while using a small deck and a bit of retention/cycle to line up good turns. It beat most hallway fights, but its defense stayed mostly basic and its final list still had filler, so it likely fell off in longer, harder combats.

energy_engine: base 3 energy
weak_points: The deck was worst at long or high-pressure fights because it lacked energy acceleration and true block scaling, while still carrying starter filler that reduced Shiv consistency.

build_summary: This was a starter-heavy Silent control deck that tried to reduce incoming damage with repeated Weak from Neutralize+, Leg Sweep, and Piercing Wail, then win through chip damage and a very small poison package. Because so much of its output still came from Strike and Defend, it never found enough scaling or consistency to get through the boss.

defense_engine: Basic Defends and Survivor backed by Leg Sweep block and heavy Weak application, with Piercing Wail as emergency mitigation.
cycle_engine: not observed
energy_engine: base 3 energy
weak_points: The deck was worst at closing fights efficiently: it had too many starter cards, almost no draw or energy support, and only a thin poison package, so it fell behind against a boss-level HP pool.

key_cards.insight: It was the deck's best true defensive pickup, providing 126 traceable block while repeatedly applying Weak.
Upgraded Neutralize was a major part of the control plan, being played often and supplying a large share of the deck's Weak.
This was the only clearly supported damage card in the final deck, contributing direct damage while starting poison stacks.
Even without traceable block numbers, four exhaust triggers suggest it was an important panic button for surviving hard enemy turns.
As a lone poison amplifier in a deck with very little poison support, it did not have enough surrounding cards to become a reliable win condition.
Strike leading total damage at 130 is a red flag that the deck's offense stayed underdeveloped and overly dependent on starter cards.

build_summary: This was a small Silent deck centered on generated Shivs and frequent hand-cycling, using Predator and Memento Mori as secondary damage while Noxious Fumes offered a slow backup scaling line. It stayed alive with basic block cards and double Piercing Wail, but the final list was still a bit mixed and under-supported for explosive late fights.
damage_engine: Generated Shivs were the main kill tool by a wide margin, apparently boosted by incidental Strength gains; Predator and Memento Mori provided the next most important direct damage, with Noxious Fumes as secondary slow scaling.
defense_engine: Mostly Defend/Survivor plus Cloak and Dagger and Finesse for block, backed by double Piercing Wail for large enemy damage reduction turns.
cycle_engine: Prepared+ + Calculated Gamble + Acrobatics+ with Predator's next-turn draw (inferred from card effects and play patterns).
energy_engine: Base 3 energy; no reliable extra energy source was present in the final deck.
weak_points: It struggled with fast, high-pressure encounters because its defense was mostly fair-value blocking and Piercing Wail, while its damage package was split across Shiv, poison, and off-plan attacks without strong energy or scaling support.

With 29 plays, it was one of the most-used cards and helped keep the small deck moving toward its better hands.

build_summary: This was a medium-large Silent deck centered on generating lots of Shivs and cycling hard to find its cheap attacks, with Murder and Memento Mori acting as secondary burst damage. It defended by stacking Dexterity and soft-locking enemy turns with Piercing Wail rather than by pure poison stall.
damage_engine: Shiv generation from Blade Dance and Cloak and Dagger drove most of the damage, with Murder and Memento Mori providing notable follow-up burst; Noxious Fumes looked like minor side damage rather than the main kill plan.
defense_engine: Footwork+ amplified Survivor, Finesse, and Cloak and Dagger blocks, while double Piercing Wail carried a lot of survivability by cutting enemy damage on key turns.
cycle_engine: Acrobatics, Acrobatics+, Prepared+, Calculated Gamble, and Predator suggest a strong inferred draw/discard shell for finding payoff cards quickly.
energy_engine: Mostly base 3 energy; Tactician+ suggests occasional discard-based energy spikes, but no direct energy trace was reported.
weak_points: The deck was somewhat unfocused and 29 cards large, so its scaling and payoff density were inconsistent when the Shiv/cycle package did not come together quickly.

key_cards.insight: With Shiv as the top damage source by a wide margin, Blade Dance was a central enabler of the deck's main attack pattern.
It fit the deck perfectly by contributing both Shiv generation and block, bridging offense and defense in the same card.
The single Dexterity spike noticeably improved the deck's otherwise modest block cards and made its defensive turns more reliable.
Enemy Strength reduction from Piercing Wail was one of the most impactful defensive effects actually observed in the boss fight.
The Acrobatics package was a key inferred reason the deck could dig to Shivs, Piercing Wail, and discard payoffs consistently.
It showed up as a meaningful secondary damage source, giving the deck burst beyond its steady Shiv chip.
Although not directly traceable in the report, it is the clearest energy card in the list and likely paired with Prepared/Acrobatics/Calculated Gamble.
An X-cost finisher did not match the deck's mostly cheap, cycling Shiv shell very well and did not appear among the notable traced outputs.

coherence_analysis: The deck had a real Shiv-plus-cycle core with Footwork and Piercing Wail supporting it well, but several isolated cards like Skewer+, lone Noxious Fumes, and other one-ofs diluted the overall focus.

build_summary: This was a Silent tempo deck built around shiv burst turns, using discard/filtering to assemble efficient hands and convert cheap attacks into steady damage. It blocked with Leg Sweep, Cloak and Dagger, and a lot of basic defenses, then tried to spike turns with double Adrenaline and retention from Well-Laid Plans, but it lacked strong long-fight scaling.
damage_engine: Shivs from Blade Dance and Cloak and Dagger were the main engine, backed by Dagger Throw chip; combat logs also show a sizable amount of temporary/generated Pinpoint+ damage.
defense_engine: Leg Sweep plus Cloak and Dagger, Survivor, and mostly basic Defends.
cycle_engine: Inferred discard/filter package from Dagger Throw + Prepared+ + Reflex+ + Tools of the Trade, with Well-Laid Plans providing retention rather than draw.
energy_engine: Double Adrenaline+ on top of base 3 energy.
weak_points: It was worst at long boss fights, where shiv burst and weak-based defense were not enough and the deck's remaining starter cards plus limited scaling caused it to run out of impact.

key_cards.insight: It was the cleanest source of the 111 Shiv plays, and Shiv was the deck's largest measured damage source at 492.
It was excellent glue, generating 196 traceable block while also feeding the Shiv plan.
It dealt 373 traceable damage and was the clearest discard enabler for Reflex+ and hand smoothing.
This was the deck's strongest non-basic defensive card, contributing 173 block and repeated Weak application.
The two upgraded copies supplied most of the deck's extra energy, with +38 traceable energy enabling explosive turns.
It tied together Prepared+, Reflex+, and Dagger Throw by turning awkward hands into filtered ones every turn.
Retention helped line up Adrenaline, Leg Sweep, and burst pieces for stronger setup and payoff turns.
Despite the upgrade investment, there is no clear traceable payoff from it, suggesting it was too slow or lacked premium targets often enough to matter.

coherence_analysis: The shiv package and discard/filter package fit together reasonably well, but the deck stayed somewhat diluted by starter cards and lacked a truly reliable scaling finisher, so its synergy was real but incomplete.

build_summary: This was a Silent control-oriented hybrid deck that tried to survive with cheap block, Weak, and Strength reduction while winning through a mix of direct attack damage and some poison. It was effective in normal fights, but the final list never fully consolidated around one scaling kill plan.
damage_engine: Mixed chip damage from Eradicate and Predator+, with supplemental Poison from Deadly Poison/Deadly Poison+ and some Shiv burst from Blade Dance+.
defense_engine: Primarily Defend/Defend+ plus Survivor, Dodge and Roll, and Escape Plan, backed by Neutralize+, double Piercing Wail, and Malaise-style enemy damage reduction.
cycle_engine: Inferred light cycling from Acrobatics, Calculated Gamble+, Escape Plan, and Predator+'s next-turn draw, with Well-Laid Plans helping hand selection.
energy_engine: Adrenaline+ only; otherwise mostly base 3 energy.
weak_points: It lacked a focused late-fight finisher and still carried too much starter filler, so long boss combats could outscale its split damage plan.

build_summary: This looked like a half-formed Shiv/discard midrange Silent deck: it chipped enemies down with generated Shivs and Pinpoint while trying to stay alive through Afterimage, Backflip, and a heavy base of starter block cards. The draw/discard shell was present, but the 29-card list stayed bloated enough that the engine never became consistent.
damage_engine: Generated Shivs were the main damage source, backed by Pinpoint as the biggest non-generated hitter and supplemented by basic attacks like Strike, Dagger Throw, Neutralize+, and Poisoned Stab; poison was only a minor side package.
defense_engine: Mostly basic Defends/Survivor/Backflip, with Afterimage adding small incremental block on card-spam and Shiv turns.
cycle_engine: Inferred draw/discard package from Backflip + Acrobatics+ + Prepared+ + Dagger Throw, with Well-Laid Plans smoothing awkward hands.
energy_engine: Tactician enabled by discard outlets like Prepared+, Acrobatics+, and Dagger Throw; otherwise mostly base 3 energy.
weak_points: It was worst at converting its discard/cycle pieces into reliable scaling, so the bloated deck often drew too many starters and could neither block hard enough nor kill quickly in tougher fights.

key_cards.insight: This was the clearest synergy card in the deck, turning cheap/generated plays into defense and explaining the traceable block coming from Shiv.

coherence_analysis: The deck had real overlap between Shiv generation, Afterimage, and a light discard/cycle shell, but too many starter cards and mixed standalone attacks kept it from becoming a focused or reliable engine.

build_summary: This was a shiv-heavy Silent deck that tried to win by flooding cheap attacks, especially Shivs from Blade Dance and Cloak and Dagger, while Phantom Blades and Envenom made each hit more threatening. It had some draw/discard to keep turns flowing, but its defense stayed mostly on starter block cards plus Piercing Wail, so longer boss fights exposed the build.
damage_engine: Shiv generation was the main damage engine, with Shiv dealing 494 traceable damage; Blade Dance and Cloak and Dagger fed that plan, while Phantom Blades and Envenom appear to have boosted repeated small hits, and Predator+, Echoing Slash, Slice, and Pinpoint+ filled in secondary damage.
defense_engine: Mostly basic block from Defend, Survivor, and Cloak and Dagger, backed up by Piercing Wail's Strength reduction and some temporary protection from Untouchable.
cycle_engine: Inferred draw/discard package from Acrobatics+, Prepared+, Calculated Gamble, Expertise, and Predator+'s next-turn draw.
energy_engine: base 3 energy
weak_points: It was worst at sustained boss fights, where limited block scaling, no extra energy, and leftover starter cards made it hard to both set up and survive once burst damage stopped ending fights quickly.

key_cards.insight: This appears to be the deck's main burst enabler, repeatedly applied and then consumed by attacks/Shivs, making it central to how the deck converted many small hits into real damage.
Poison being applied off Shiv, Slice, Echoing Slash, and Neutralize+ shows this power turned the attack spam package into a poison-backed damage engine.
Even without direct traceable damage on the card itself, it was a key Shiv generator for the run's biggest damage source by far.
It contributed 137 traceable block while also feeding the Shiv plan, making it one of the cleanest synergy cards in the deck.
It provided 115 traceable damage and repeatedly granted next-turn draw, helping the deck refill for burst turns.
Large repeated Strength reduction was important support for a deck whose normal block package was otherwise pretty thin.

The remaining basic Strike had no standout traceable contribution and represented starter clutter the deck never fully cleaned up.

coherence_analysis: The deck had a real throughline in Shiv spam plus Phantom Blades/Envenom support and some cycle, but it still carried too many starter blocks, lacked energy support, and never built a strong enough defensive scaling package for long fights.

build_summary: This was a hybrid Silent midrange deck that tried to win by layering modest poison with solid front-loaded attacks while staying alive through repeated block turns and card cycling. It was competent through normal fights, but the deck stayed too split between poison, Shiv chip, and basic attack damage to scale cleanly into the late game.
damage_engine: Poison via Noxious Fumes+ / Deadly Poison / Bouncing Flask+ backed by front-loaded attacks from Predator+, Assassinate, and Blade Dance; notably, a lot of total damage still came from Strike/Strike+.
defense_engine: Mostly skill-based blocking from Mirage+, Dodge and Roll, Survivor, Piercing Wail, and still-significant reliance on basic Defends.
cycle_engine: Inferred draw/cycle from double Acrobatics+ plus Predator+ next-turn draw, with Tactician+ as a discard payoff.
energy_engine: No strong permanent engine observed in the final deck; mostly base energy, with only minor inferred support from Tactician+ (earlier in the run, Production cards supplied traceable extra energy).
weak_points: It was worst at closing long, high-pressure fights because its scaling was light and fragmented, so it still leaned too much on Strikes and medium-impact attacks instead of a single strong endgame plan.

build_summary: This was a small Silent deck trying to play a tempo attack-and-cycle game, using upgraded utility cards to sift hands and land efficient attacks. It never found strong scaling or durable defense, so it leaned heavily on starters and ran out of steam against the boss.

build_summary: This deck looked like a light poison-leaning Silent build backed by efficient single-hit attacks and a few utility tools, trying to win through steady chip damage rather than heavy scaling. build_summary: This Silent deck was a hybrid tempo build centered on frequent Shiv production and cheap attack pressure, with Accuracy+ giving the Shiv plan real scaling and poison serving as a backup boss-damage package. energy_engine: Adrenaline and Charge Battery, otherwise mostly base 3 energy.
weak_points: It was worst at surviving boss-level pressure because the deck split between shivs, generic attacks, and poison without enough block scaling or a single fully committed late-game plan.

Provided a crucial mix of block and Shiv generation to support the Envenom strategy.

coherence_analysis: The core Envenom and Shiv synergy is solid, but the deck's potential is severely bottlenecked by zero card draw and too many starter cards.

build_summary: A hybrid deck utilizing a discard engine to cycle through a split damage package of poison and shivs.

cycle_engine: Acrobatics and Tools of the Trade.
energy_engine: Tactician triggered by Acrobatics, Survivor, or Tools of the Trade.
weak_points: Lacked a focused scaling win condition, splitting its damage output inefficiently between poison and shivs.

build_summary: A barebones Act 1 deck that relied on a few premium attacks to survive early combats but failed to develop a cohesive strategy or scaling.

defense_engine: Basic Defends and Survivor.

energy_engine: Base 3 energy.
weak_points: Lacked AoE damage and defensive scaling, making the multi-enemy Act 1 boss impossible to survive.

An efficient early-game attack that applied minor poison to supplement damage.

coherence_analysis: While Predator and Poisoned Stab+ are strong early picks, the deck failed to find any synergistic scaling, AoE, or defensive engines before the boss.

build_summary: A thin, Shiv-focused deck relying on Cloak and Dagger and Blade Dance for consistent damage output, supplemented by Ultimate Strike.
damage_engine: Shivs generated by Cloak and Dagger and Blade Dance, alongside upfront damage from Ultimate Strike+.
defense_engine: Basic Defends, Survivor, and incidental block from Cloak and Dagger.
cycle_engine: Acrobatics and Dagger Throw
energy_engine: none observed
weak_points: Lacks late-game scaling for both damage (e.g., Accuracy) and defense (e.g., Footwork), which would likely cause issues in Act 3.

key_cards.insight: Provided both block and Shivs, serving as a highly played, dual-purpose engine for the deck.
A primary Shiv generator that fueled the deck's burst damage.
Provided solid upfront damage and was highly upgraded/played, though its scaling was slightly counteracted by removing basic Strikes.
Provided necessary card draw to keep the thin deck cycling efficiently.
Crucial defensive utility for surviving multi-attack turns and mitigating heavy damage.

coherence_analysis: The deck is thin and effectively focused on Shiv generation, but lacks the scaling powers needed to close out a full run.

build_summary: This deck heavily leaned into generating and scaling Shivs for both offense and defense. By utilizing multiple upgraded Cloak and Daggers alongside Blade Dances and scaling them with two upgraded Accuracies, it achieved a steady, high-damage output.
damage_engine: Massive Shiv generation via Blade Dance+ and Cloak and Dagger+, scaled by double Accuracy+.
defense_engine: Cloak and Dagger+ and Backflip, enhanced by Footwork, with Piercing Wail for mitigating large multi-hit attacks.
cycle_engine: Acrobatics+ and Backflip provided the necessary draw to keep the Shiv generators flowing.
energy_engine: None observed; relied on low-cost cards and base energy.
weak_points: The deck lacks large burst block for surviving massive single-hit attacks, relying mostly on Piercing Wail and incremental block.

key_cards.insight: Having two of these upgraded provided the critical damage scaling needed to make Shivs lethal.
With three upgraded copies, it served as the primary engine for both generating block and fueling the Shiv offense.
Generated large bursts of zero-cost attacks to synergize with Accuracy.
The most played card by far, acting as the primary damage payload.
Provided vital damage mitigation against multi-hit attacks, covering the deck's weakness to massive incoming damage.
Helped cycle through the deck to find Accuracy and more Shiv generators while providing block.
Scaled the block from Cloak and Dagger+, Backflip, and Dodge and Roll+ to ensure consistent defense.

coherence_analysis: Highly synergistic shiv-focused build with clear damage scaling and consistent block generation, though still carrying slightly bulky starter cards.

build_summary: An early-game Silent deck that relied entirely on basic cards and early Shiv generation, failing to find enough damage or defense to survive the Act 1 Boss.
damage_engine: Shivs (likely generated by Up My Sleeve) and basic Strikes, with a minor contribution from Poisoned Stab.
defense_engine: Basic Defends and Neutralize's weak application.
cycle_engine: Two copies of Acrobatics provided card draw.
energy_engine: base 3 energy
weak_points: The deck completely lacked damage scaling and defensive power, making the Act 1 Boss a pure stat check it could not pass.

key_cards.insight: Generated a massive amount of Shivs, acting as the primary source of offensive output.
Enabled card cycling to churn through the starter deck and find playable damage.
Provided minor supplemental damage and poison application.
The only reliable source of block, which was heavily played but insufficient for boss scaling.
Played 25 times simply due to lack of better options.
Added late and failed to make a significant impact on the run's outcome.

coherence_analysis: While Acrobatics provided good cycle, the deck was entirely missing the scaling damage or block engines required to clear Act 1.

build_summary: A highly cycled, thin hybrid deck that split its offensive output between Shiv generation and Poison scaling while defending heavily through Dexterity and card draw.
damage_engine: Shivs via Blade Dance and passive Poison scaling via Noxious Fumes and Serpent Form
defense_engine: Footwork scaling combined with Backflips, supported by Malaise and Piercing Wail+
cycle_engine: Backflip, Acrobatics, and Reflex+
energy_engine: none observed
weak_points: The split offensive focus between shivs and poison lacked the necessary exponential scaling or focused burst to quickly close out the Act 3 boss.

build_summary: A focused Shiv deck that relied on generating and playing a massive number of Shivs for consistent damage output. It utilized cards like Cloak and Dagger+ and Flick-Flack+ alongside decent card draw to maintain momentum.
damage_engine: Shivs generated by Cloak and Dagger+, Flick-Flack+, and Hidden Daggers
defense_engine: Cloak and Dagger+, Backflip, and basic Defends
cycle_engine: Backflip, Prepared+, Acrobatics, and Adrenaline
energy_engine: Adrenaline
weak_points: Lacked strong damage scaling like Accuracy, relying purely on the sheer volume of Shivs played to defeat enemies.

key_cards.insight: Provided essential block while fueling the deck's primary damage engine with Shivs.
A primary source of Shiv generation, played frequently to maintain damage output.
Contributed significantly to the Shiv generation engine.
Provided crucial card draw and block to keep the deck cycling smoothly.

build_summary: A highly focused, thin Shiv deck that relies on Accuracy for damage scaling and Backflips for consistent card draw and block.

defense_engine: Backflips, Dodge and Roll+, and basic Defends.
cycle_engine: Multiple Backflips providing consistent card draw.
energy_engine: Base energy (no energy generation cards observed).
weak_points: Lack of energy generation makes playing high-cost cards like Nightmare clunky, and remaining basic Strikes dilute the draw pool.

key_cards.insight: The primary generator of Shivs, fueling the deck's main damage engine.

build_summary: An early run Silent deck that attempted to lean into Shiv generation and upfront damage. The run ended abruptly against a multi-enemy Elite before the deck could find sufficient scaling or AoE.
damage_engine: Shivs generated by Leading Strike and Hidden Daggers, supplemented by Predator and basic Strikes.

cycle_engine: Predator provided some next-turn draw.
energy_engine: base 3 energy
weak_points: The deck lacked multi-target damage and strong defensive scaling, leaving it vulnerable to the Phantasmal Gardener elite.

Played out of necessity, diluting draws from the more impactful added cards.

coherence_analysis: The deck had the beginnings of a Shiv package but was heavily bogged down by starter cards and lacked a cohesive way to handle tough multi-enemy encounters.

defense_engine: Basic Defend cards and Dash

build_summary: A focused Shiv deck relying on Blade Dance and Fan of Knives, scaled by Accuracy for damage.

Crucial for surviving multi-attacks and mitigating heavy incoming damage.

build_summary: This deck attempted a slow, defensive strategy relying on double Footwork to scale block while dealing chip damage with Shivs and Noxious Fumes. However, the split focus between weak Shivs and slow poison left it without enough damage scaling to defeat the Act 2 boss.
damage_engine: Shivs (generated heavily during the run) and passive poison from Noxious Fumes, supplemented by basic Strikes.
defense_engine: Double Footwork scaling basic Defends, alongside Leg Sweep, Blur, and Piercing Wail.
cycle_engine: Minimal cycle observed, relying slightly on Dagger Throw+.
energy_engine: Accelerant and base energy.
weak_points: The deck lacked strong damage scaling or burst, making it too slow to outpace the scaling threats of the Act 2 boss.

key_cards.insight: Having two copies provided the core defensive scaling needed to survive Elites and reach Act 2.
Provided necessary passive damage scaling for longer fights, though it was too slow on its own.
Generated the bulk of the Shivs played during the run to provide consistent chip damage.
A strong mitigation tool that synergized perfectly with the defensive, slow-paced game plan.
Provided occasional energy bursts to help play out the defensive hand.
Still heavily relied upon (played 40 times) despite being highly inefficient in Act 2.

coherence_analysis: Strong defensive synergy with double Footwork and solid block cards, but the damage engine was unfocused, splitting between unscaled Shivs and slow poison.

build_summary: A highly synergistic Shiv deck relying on multiple Shiv generators combined with Accuracy and Phantom Blades for damage, while using Afterimage for scaling passive defense.
damage_engine: Shiv generation (Blade Dance, Up My Sleeve) heavily scaled by Accuracy, Phantom Blades, and Vajra, with Finisher as a massive payoff.
defense_engine: Afterimage triggering passive block on every Shiv played, supplemented by Dexterity (Footwork) buffing standard block cards like Backflip.
cycle_engine: Draw from Acrobatics, Prepared, Adrenaline, and Backflip to cycle back to Shiv generators.
energy_engine: Adrenaline, Production, and Nunchaku triggering frequently off Shiv plays.
weak_points: Heavy reliance on playing numerous attacks per turn makes the deck vulnerable to multi-play punishers like thorns or Time Eater mechanics, though Afterimage mitigates this slightly.

key_cards.insight: Transforms Shivs from marginal 4-damage attacks into highly efficient 10+ damage nukes.
Provided an immense amount of block simply by executing the deck's primary Shiv strategy.

A zero-cost enabler that fuels the opening turns with energy and draw.
An isolated poison card in a deck entirely dedicated to direct Shiv damage.

coherence_analysis: The deck has incredibly tight synergy between Shiv generation, damage scaling, and on-play block generation, hindered only slightly by a few orphaned non-Shiv cards like Haze and Catastrophe.

build_summary: A highly synergistic Shiv deck that leveraged attack-scaling relics (Kunai, Ornamental Fan, Miniature Cannon) and Afterimage to generate massive damage and block from 0-cost attacks.
damage_engine: Shivs enhanced by Phantom Blades and Miniature Cannon, backed by Finisher for burst payoffs.
defense_engine: Passive block from Afterimage and Ornamental Fan, amplified by Dexterity scaling from Kunai.
cycle_engine: Inferred from Acrobatics, Adrenaline, and Expertise providing heavy draw and discard to sustain combos.
energy_engine: Adrenaline, supported by Pumpkin Candle and Candelabra.
weak_points: The deck has a bloated size with basic Defends and multiple disparate sub-themes (Poison, Sly) that could disrupt drawing core Shiv components.

key_cards.insight: Provides the sheer volume of 0-cost attacks needed to power up Kunai, Ornamental Fan, and Miniature Cannon.
Transforms Shivs from incremental pokes into massive damage spikes while allowing strategic hoarding.
Essential passive defense that triggers automatically during the deck's high-action offensive turns.
Provides the crucial card flow needed to sustain long combo chains and triggers the deck's Sly cards.
Acts as the primary burst damage finisher after extending turns with multiple Shiv plays.
Dual-purpose enabler that stabilizes early turns with block while feeding the Shiv engine.
Free energy and draw that jumpstarts the deck's engine without slowing down momentum.
Basic attacks that should have been prioritized for removal to ensure faster access to the Shiv engine.

coherence_analysis: Exceptional synergy between high-volume Shiv generation and attack-scaling relics, though a few stray cards slightly dilute the main engine.

build_summary: A high-frequency attack deck leveraging Shiv generation and the Phantom Blades power for retention and burst damage, supported by a heavy discard-draw engine.
damage_engine: Shiv-based attack spam (Phantom Blades + Blade Dance) with multi-hit payoffs (Exterminate, Ricochet)
defense_engine: Double-block scaling (Shadowmeld) and tactical strength reduction (Piercing Wail)
cycle_engine: Inferred discard-draw synergy (Acrobatics, Prepared, Dagger Throw) triggering Sly cards (Reflex)
energy_engine: Delayed energy scaling (Pael's Flesh) and conditional gain (Art of War)
weak_points: The deck suffers from a mechanical conflict between Art of War and high-frequency Shiv plays, alongside a lack of immediate energy generation to set up its multi-card combos.

key_cards.insight: Transforms Shivs from incidental damage into a scalable win condition with Retain.
Provides the raw volume of attacks needed for Kusarigama triggers and Phantom Blades value.
Crucial for survival, allowing basic Defends and Escape Plan to reach acceptable block thresholds.
The primary enabler for the deck's discard-synergy cards like Reflex and Shadow Step.
Frequently disabled by the deck's reliance on playing multiple Shivs per turn.
Essential for stripping enemy defenses and applying Vulnerable to maximize multi-hit attack damage.

coherence_analysis: The deck has strong synergy between its Shiv generation and discard engine, but it is held back by the anti-synergy of Art of War and potentially slow setup times.

build_summary: A focused Shiv deck utilizing Accuracy and Phantom Blades for scaling damage, supported by Footwork and Paper Krane for defense.
damage_engine: shiv generation + accuracy
defense_engine: dexterity + weak
cycle_engine: acrobatics + backflip
energy_engine: base energy
weak_points: Lacked reliable energy generation to play the high volume of cards drawn, and may have struggled against enemies with artifact or scaling damage.

key_cards.insight: Defined the damage strategy by scaling all Shivs.
Provided a massive burst payoff for exhausting Shivs throughout the combat.
Scaled block generation to keep up with enemy damage.
Provided massive block and applied Weak, which was highly effective with Paper Krane.
Provided essential card draw to find key powers and Shiv generators.
A flat damage card that anti-synergizes with the large hand sizes generated by Acrobatics and Ring of the Snake.

coherence_analysis: Strong synergy between Shiv generation, Accuracy, and Knife Trap, with solid defensive scaling via Footwork and Weak, though hindered by retained Strikes and Precise Cut.

build_summary: A highly synergistic Shiv deck that leveraged multiple Blade Dances and Cloak and Daggers alongside Accuracy and Phantom Blades for damage. Two innate Afterimages provided massive passive block generation for every card played.
damage_engine: Shiv generation (Blade Dance, Cloak and Dagger, Fan of Knives) scaled by Accuracy and Phantom Blades
defense_engine: Card spam triggering double innate Afterimage, supported by Footwork and Cloak and Dagger

key_cards.insight: Having two innate copies defined the defensive strategy, turning every offensive Shiv into block.
Essential scaling that made the 0-cost Shivs hit hard enough to win.
The primary fuel for the deck, generating 3 Shivs per cast to trigger all synergies.
Provided additional scaling and utility by allowing Shivs to be retained for burst turns.
Perfect draw engine for a deck that rapidly empties its hand of 0-cost cards.
Scaled the block from Cloak and Dagger and Survivor to supplement Afterimage.
An expensive attack that diluted the zero-cost spam strategy and competed for limited base energy.

coherence_analysis: Extremely strong synergy between Shiv generation, Accuracy, and Afterimage, though a few heavy attacks like Volley slightly dilute the zero-cost spam strategy.

build_summary: This deck attempted to hybridize a poison-scaling strategy with a discard and Sly attack engine.
damage_engine: Poison application paired with Sly attack triggers and Tingsha relic damage
defense_engine: Basic block cards and Piercing Wail, supplemented by Pael's Legion and Parrying Shield
cycle_engine: Discard and draw package (Acrobatics, Prepared, Tools of the Trade, Backflip)
energy_engine: base energy
weak_points: The deck split its focus between two distinct damage engines (poison and discard/Sly) while failing to remove basic strikes, leading to poor consistency and draw dilution.

key_cards.insight: Provided the essential poison stacks required for Accelerant to function.
The critical burst damage payoff for the poison investment.
Capitalized on the deck's discard engine to provide free area-of-effect damage.
Acted as a consistent passive engine for triggering Tingsha and Sly mechanics.
Fueled the deck's cycle while selectively enabling Sly payoffs.
Provided critical multi-enemy damage mitigation to survive tough encounters.
With 5 copies still in the deck, they heavily bogged down the draws needed to find poison or discard pieces.

coherence_analysis: While the discard/Sly and poison packages each had strong internal synergies, running both simultaneously alongside 11 unupgraded starter cards created significant draw inconsistency.

build_summary: A Shiv-centric deck utilizing Phantom Blades and Fan of Knives to scale damage and provide area-of-effect coverage.

defense_engine: Static block and Weak application (Leg Sweep + Orichalcum)
cycle_engine: Low-impact draw (Expertise + Ring of the Snake)
energy_engine: base energy
weak_points: Low front-loaded block and lack of energy generation to support high-cost defensive cards alongside Shiv setups.

build_summary: A highly synergistic poison deck that used Accelerant to multiply poison damage, supported by a robust discard engine for card draw and energy generation.
damage_engine: Poison application (Corrosive Wave, Bubble Bubble, Noxious Fumes) multiplied by Accelerant.
defense_engine: Weakness application (Malaise, Leg Sweep) combined with Dexterity (Anticipate) and standard block cards.
cycle_engine: Discard cycling (Calculated Gamble, Tools of the Trade) and raw draw (Backflip, Expertise).
energy_engine: Discarding Tactician for bonus energy.
weak_points: Vulnerable to enemies with Scrutiny (anti-draw) or Grasp (energy drain), which temporarily shut down the deck's heavy cycling and energy requirements.

key_cards.insight: Defined the damage scaling by triggering poison multiple times per turn, turning moderate poison stacks into lethal damage.
Synergized perfectly with the deck's heavy draw to stack massive poison efficiently.
Enabled massive draw turns, fueling Corrosive Wave and finding key defensive cards.
Provided crucial energy when discarded, allowing the deck to play expensive hands despite energy drains.
Crucial for surviving the boss's high-strength multi-attacks by stripping Strength and applying Weak.
Provided consistent, free discard triggers every turn to activate Sly effects like Flick-Flack and Tactician.

coherence_analysis: Extremely tight synergy between poison application, draw-triggered effects (Corrosive Wave), and discard-based energy/cycle (Calculated Gamble, Tactician).

build_summary: A highly synergistic Shiv and Poison hybrid deck that utilized mass Shiv generation to trigger Afterimage for block, and Envenom alongside Snecko Skull to stack lethal poison.
damage_engine: Mass Shiv generation scaled directly by Accuracy++ and Tracking++, and indirectly via Envenom++ and Snecko Skull applying poison.
defense_engine: Afterimage++ providing constant passive block from playing numerous 0-cost Shivs.
cycle_engine: Mad Science, Backflip, and Tools of the Trade alongside Calculated Gamble and Joss Paper for robust card cycling.
energy_engine: Base energy
weak_points: Heavy reliance on 3 Energy meant the deck was vulnerable if it couldn't find its key scaling powers (Afterimage, Envenom) quickly.

combat_summaries.enemy: multi:Scroll of Biting+Scroll of Biting+Scroll of Biting

key_cards.insight: Passively generated enough block to mitigate boss multi-attacks just by progressing the offensive gameplan.
Transformed every 0-cost attack into a dual-threat scaling engine alongside Snecko Skull.
Acted as an ultimate finisher, leveraging the entire combat's Exhaust pile to guarantee a massive lethal turn.
Provided the raw base damage required to make Shivs highly threatening before poison even ticked.
Multiplied the high base damage of Accuracy-buffed Shivs, pushing total damage per Shiv to 30+.
Enabled passive card selection and reliably triggered discard synergies like Sly on Flick-Flack and mitigated curses like Doubt.
Provided excellent burst draw and block, though its Bound keyword sometimes required careful turn sequencing.

coherence_analysis: An extremely tightly focused and synergistic hybrid deck where generation of a single token type (Shivs) simultaneously fueled defense, direct damage, and poison scaling.

build_summary: A defensive Shiv deck that relied on Dexterity scaling and damage mitigation to survive, but lacked a scaling damage engine to close out boss fights.
damage_engine: shiv generation (Blade Dance, Cloak and Dagger) and frontloaded attacks (Dash++)
defense_engine: dexterity scaling (Footwork++) combined with damage mitigation (Malaise, Piercing Wail, Neutralize++)
cycle_engine: basic draw and discard (Backflip, Dagger Throw)
energy_engine: base energy
weak_points: The deck completely lacked scaling damage, causing it to slowly die to the Lagavulin Matriarch's continuous Strength and Dexterity debuffs.

key_cards.insight: Provided essential Dexterity scaling to make basic block cards viable.
Generated Shivs for the deck's primary, albeit weak, damage output.
Consistently provided high-value block and damage, often retained for critical turns.
Effectively mitigated enemy scaling and multi-attacks by reducing Strength.
Crucial for hand manipulation, retaining key defensive cards until the enemy attacked.
Became completely useless and dealt 0 damage once the boss applied heavy Strength debuffs.

coherence_analysis: The deck had a strong defensive foundation with Footwork and mitigation tools, but completely lacked a synergistic damage payoff for its Shivs.

build_summary: A defensive Shiv deck that utilized Helical Dart and Footwork to scale Dexterity for immense block, but completely lacked a damage payoff to pass boss DPS checks.
damage_engine: incremental shivs and raw attacks without scaling enablers
defense_engine: dexterity-scaled dodge and roll combined with afterimage
cycle_engine: acrobatics and backflip chaining
energy_engine: turn 1 burst from very hot cocoa and lantern, then base energy
weak_points: The deck entirely lacked damage scaling (like Accuracy or poison), leaving it unable to burst down the Act 2 boss before being overwhelmed by the Sandpit mechanic.

key_cards.insight: Provided the foundational Dexterity required to block heavy boss multi-attacks.
Leveraged the acquired Dexterity twice over two turns for highly efficient mitigation.
Generated a Shiv for 0-energy damage while proccing the Helical Dart relic for extra Dexterity.
Used to dig 4 cards deep to find crucial block or target cards like Frantic Escape when under pressure.
Relied upon as the primary burst damage source (14 damage) due to the lack of actual scaling payoffs.
Remained unplayed and discarded during critical boss turns because it consumed too much of the base 3 energy.
Retained multiple copies late into the run, offering a terrible damage-to-energy ratio when every point of energy mattered.

coherence_analysis: The defensive engine of Shivs feeding Dexterity scaling was well-assembled, but the total absence of a damage win condition doomed the run in prolonged fights.

build_summary: A highly synergistic Shiv deck that used Accuracy and Phantom Blades to scale damage, while leveraging Runic Pyramid to hold key defensive and burst cards.
damage_engine: Shiv generation (Blade Dance, Cloak and Dagger) scaled by Accuracy and Phantom Blades, with Finisher and Pinpoint as payoffs
defense_engine: Dexterity scaling (Footwork) with block skills (Cloak and Dagger, Defend, Blur) and multi-hit mitigation (Piercing Wail)
cycle_engine: Burst draw (Master of Strategy, Adrenaline, Backflip) and hand cycling (Calculated Gamble)
energy_engine: Energy generation skills (Mad Science, Adrenaline)
weak_points: The deck relied heavily on drawing and playing its powers early; without Runic Pyramid, hand clog from situational cards could have been an issue.

combat_summaries.enemy: multi:Scroll of Biting+Scroll of Biting+Scroll of Biting

key_cards.insight: Provided the core damage scaling for the deck's primary damage source.
Added significant burst damage to the first Shiv played each turn, essential for taking down high-HP targets.
The primary engine for generating Shivs to trigger synergies and deal damage.
Acted as a massive payoff card after playing multiple Shivs, dealing huge burst damage.
Scaled Dexterity to ensure block cards could keep up with Act 3 boss damage.
Generated crucial extra energy to allow playing out large hands of drawn cards and Shiv generators.
Held with Runic Pyramid to perfectly counter multi-hit attacks from the Queen.
Allowed for full hand resets to dig for key powers or burst damage when needed.

coherence_analysis: Highly synergistic deck with strong Shiv generation, excellent power scaling, and robust energy/draw engines, perfectly complemented by Runic Pyramid.

build_summary: A high-draw Shiv and Discard deck that cycled rapidly to generate 0-cost attacks and trigger Sly effects for free AoE damage.
damage_engine: Shiv generation and targeted discard triggering Sly attacks, backed by incremental Poison.
defense_engine: Dexterity scaling and high-volume cycle into efficient block cards.
cycle_engine: Multiple upgraded Backflips and Calculated Gamble for massive hand replacement.
energy_engine: base energy
weak_points: The deck lacked extra energy generation, making it heavily reliant on drawing 0-cost cards or triggering Sly to play out its hand.

key_cards.insight: Served a dual purpose of generating 0-cost Shivs and discarding cards like Flick-Flack for free Sly damage.
Provided entirely cost-free AoE damage when discarded by the deck's multiple targeted discard outlets.
With three upgraded copies, it reliably cycled the deck to find 0-cost generators while providing necessary block.
Scaled the block from the high volume of Backflips, Defends, and Cloak and Daggers to fully mitigate large attacks.
Secured kills in longer encounters by applying scaling, block-bypassing poison damage.
Discarded entire hands of basics or unplayable cards to draw into critical powers or 0-cost generators.
Provided excellent damage mitigation by reducing enemy Strength and applying Weak, even when played for 0 energy.

coherence_analysis: Excellent synergy between heavy draw, targeted discard outlets, and Sly mechanics, though limited to base energy.

build_summary: A hybrid Shiv and Discard deck that used targeted discard to trigger Sly effects and generate Shivs, finishing enemies with Memento Mori and Finisher.
damage_engine: Shivs and Sly attacks (Flick-Flack, Ricochet) fueled by discard, with Memento Mori and Finisher as burst payoffs
defense_engine: Dexterity-scaled block (Footwork) combined with Blur for retention and Malaise/Piercing Wail for damage mitigation
cycle_engine: Calculated Gamble and Backflip for draw, Hidden Daggers for targeted discard
energy_engine: Tactician triggered via discard outlets
weak_points: Vulnerable to anti-draw and card-play limits (like Scrutiny and Grasp), which heavily taxed its cycle and 0-cost spam engines.

key_cards.insight: Generated Shivs and acted as the primary targeted discard outlet for Sly cards.
Provided crucial extra energy when discarded by Hidden Daggers or Calculated Gamble.
Delivered massive burst damage by capitalizing on the high volume of discarded cards.
Scaled the block from Backflip, Defend, and Cloak and Dagger to survive heavy boss attacks.
Permanently neutered dangerous multi-attacks from bosses like Doormaker.
Cycled the hand, triggered Memento Mori scaling, and activated Sly/Tactician.
Provided passive scaling damage that bypassed block and finished off enemies while the deck focused on defense.

coherence_analysis: Strong synergy between discard outlets (Hidden Daggers, Gamble) and Sly/discard payoffs (Tactician, Flick-Flack, Memento Mori), though slightly diluted by basic Strikes and Defends.

build_summary: A highly synergistic Shiv deck that leveraged cheap multi-card generation to simultaneously power massive passive damage scaling and relic-based defense.
damage_engine: Serpent Form passive damage and Phantom Blades burst fueled by Blade Dance, Up My Sleeve, and Storm of Steel, capped off by Finisher.
defense_engine: Ornamental Fan and Tuning Fork triggered passively via rapid Shiv and Skill plays, supplemented by Shadowmeld and Piercing Wail for huge multi-attack mitigation.
cycle_engine: Heavy draw from Acrobatics, Adrenaline, Master of Strategy, and Backflip.
energy_engine: Adrenaline and base energy, often leaving 0-cost Shivs to do the heavy lifting.
weak_points: Slightly vulnerable to heavy turn-one damage if defensive relics or key scaling powers were not drawn immediately.

key_cards.insight: Turned every single 0-cost Shiv into a lethal passive damage trigger, defining the deck's boss-killing strategy.
Ensured that even without Serpent Form active, Shiv hands could output highly efficient frontloaded damage.
The primary engine piece that provided both the skill play for Tuning Fork and the attacks for Ornamental Fan.
Served as the ultimate payoff for Shiv spam, delivering massive finishing blows.
Doubled block generation on crucial turns, allowing the deck to survive the Queen's massive 25-damage and multi-hit attacks.
Crucial for extending turns, providing both the energy to play powers and the draw to find generators.
An expensive, clunky attack that did not synergize with the high card-play volume the Shiv engine demanded.

coherence_analysis: The deck boasts an incredibly tight synergy between Shiv generation, passive relic defense, and power-based damage scaling, though a few remnant heavy attacks slightly diluted its draw consistency.

build_summary: A hybrid shiv and attack-heavy deck that attempted to use Finisher as a payoff but struggled with energy and draw consistency.
damage_engine: Shiv generation (Blade Dance, Cloak and Dagger) combined with AoE attacks (Echoing Slash, Dagger Spray).
defense_engine: Weak application (Leg Sweep, Neutralize) paired with standard block cards.
cycle_engine: Minor draw from Backflip and Ring of the Snake.
energy_engine: base energy
weak_points: Lack of energy and draw meant the deck couldn't reliably line up its Shiv generators with its Finisher payoff.

key_cards.insight: The primary source of burst damage via Shiv generation.
Failed to find value in the boss fight due to poor draw timing and lack of energy.
Provided massive damage mitigation by applying Weak to the boss before multi-attack turns.
Effectively cleared low-HP minions and provided AoE damage.
Provided alternative scaling damage against the boss, though it lacked synergy with the Shivs.

coherence_analysis: The deck had a clear Shiv foundation but suffered from conflicting poison cards and a complete inability to enable its Finisher payoff.

build_summary: A Shiv-focused deck utilizing Blade Dance to generate multiple attacks, paying off with Finisher for burst damage.
damage_engine: Shiv generation (Blade Dance) fueling attack-count payoffs (Finisher)
defense_engine: Weak application (Neutralize, Leg Sweep) and multi-hit mitigation (Piercing Wail)
cycle_engine: Minor draw from Backflip, Dagger Throw, and Expertise
energy_engine: Ice Cream conserving base energy
weak_points: The deck lacked consistent block scaling and sustain, leading to taking lethal damage against the Act 1 Boss's high incoming damage turns when defensive cards weren't drawn.

key_cards.insight: Generated the bulk of attacks to fuel Finisher.
Dealt 36 and 32 damage in single hits after Shivs.
Negated a 5x3 multi-attack from the Kin Priest.
Provided crucial Weak and block against high-damage enemies.
Added but rarely got value due to lack of discard enablers.
Drawn to refill hand when facing lethal damage, though it wasn't enough to save the run.

coherence_analysis: Strong offensive synergy between Blade Dance and Finisher, but defensive gaps and lack of discard enablers for Memento Mori held it back.

build_summary: A heavily committed Shiv deck utilizing Accuracy to scale damage from multiple Shiv generators, but lacking the reliable card draw and defensive engines needed to survive sustained boss attacks.
damage_engine: Shiv generation (Blade Dance, Up My Sleeve, Fan of Knives) amplified by flat damage scaling (Accuracy)
defense_engine: Strength reduction (Piercing Wail) and basic block cards (Defend, Survivor), supported by temporary Weak applications
cycle_engine: Sparse draw from Backflip and Accuracy's initial play
energy_engine: base energy
weak_points: The deck lacked reliable block scaling and draw consistency, leading to fatal hands full of strikes and basic defends when facing heavy multi-hit attacks.

key_cards.insight: Provided the essential +4 damage scaling that allowed the deck's many Shivs to meaningfully threaten enemies.
Generated massive value per energy spent once Accuracy was in play.
A reliable Shiv generator that offset its initial cost by becoming cheaper across the combat.
Provided strong AoE Shiv utility and burst damage, though exhausting as a Power limited its sustained use.
The most effective defensive card in the deck, dramatically blunting the Act 1 Boss's heavy multi-attacks.
Provided essential, albeit insufficient, card draw and cycle to locate key defensive pieces.
Despite Strike Dummy adding damage, carrying 5 basic Strikes heavily diluted the draw pool, causing critical defensive whiffs against the boss.

coherence_analysis: The offensive Shiv synergies were tightly assembled, but the deck was fatally compromised by starter card bloat and an extreme deficit in defensive scaling and card cycle.

build_summary: A highly focused Shiv deck utilizing Fan of Knives for AoE and Finisher for massive single-target burst, supported by Joss Paper for card draw.
damage_engine: Shiv generation + Fan of Knives (AoE) + Finisher (scaling burst) + Expose (Vulnerable)
defense_engine: Footwork (Dexterity) + cheap block cards (Deflect, Cloak and Dagger)
cycle_engine: Joss Paper (relic) triggering off Shiv exhausts, plus Predator and Dagger Throw
energy_engine: Pumpkin Candle (+1 energy/turn)
weak_points: The deck relies heavily on playing many cards per turn, making it vulnerable to enemies that punish card plays like Time Eater or Spiker.

key_cards.insight: Transformed single-target Shivs into AoE damage, essential for clearing multi-enemy encounters.
Capitalized on high attack counts from Shivs to deal massive burst damage.
Removed enemy block and applied Vulnerable, significantly amplifying Shiv and Finisher damage.
Provided essential Dexterity scaling to make basic block cards effective.
Efficiently generated 3 Shivs to trigger exhausts and scale Finisher.
Played only when no other options were available; should have been removed to improve consistency.

coherence_analysis: The deck is tightly focused on Shiv generation and Finisher payoffs, with excellent synergy between Fan of Knives, Expose, and Joss Paper, though it still retains some basic Strikes.

build_summary: A Shiv-focused deck utilizing Accuracy and Envenom to scale damage from multiple Shiv generators, relying on Apparitions and Dexterity for survival.
damage_engine: Shiv generation (Blade Dance, Cloak and Dagger, Leading Strike) scaled by Accuracy and Envenom.
defense_engine: Intangible from Apparitions combined with Dexterity-scaled block cards (Dodge and Roll, Defend).
cycle_engine: Calculated Gamble and Backflip for hand cycling, supplemented by the Joss Paper relic.
energy_engine: Pumpkin Candle relic providing +1 energy per turn.
weak_points: Struggled against massive multi-attacks when Apparitions were not drawn or exhausted, eventually falling to the Amalgam's scaling damage.

key_cards.insight: Boosted Shiv damage significantly, essential for the deck's primary damage output.
Provided secondary scaling via Poison, stacking quickly with the high volume of Shivs.
The primary and most efficient Shiv generator in the deck.
Crucial for surviving high-damage turns, though limited in number and exhaustible.
Allowed digging through the deck to find necessary block or burst damage without costing energy.
Capitalized on high-attack turns for massive single-target burst damage.
A curse that caused chip damage and took up hand space, contributing to HP loss.

coherence_analysis: Highly synergistic Shiv engine with strong scaling from Accuracy and Envenom, but limited defensive longevity once Apparitions were exhausted.

build_summary: A poison deck that heavily utilized discard mechanics to trigger Sly effects, generating free draw, defense, and AoE poison.
damage_engine: Poison application (Deadly Poison++, Bouncing Flask) and AoE Sly poison (Haze++ via discard)
defense_engine: Dexterity scaling (Abrasive via Sly) combined with standard block cards (Dodge and Roll++, Cloak and Dagger)
cycle_engine: Discard-triggered draw (Reflex++ via Prepared and Survivor)
energy_engine: Free card plays via Sly mechanics
weak_points: The deck relied on drawing specific discard enablers alongside Sly cards, which could stall without Well-Laid Plans active.

key_cards.insight: Applied massive AoE poison for free when discarded, serving as a primary damage source in multi-enemy fights.
Provided massive 0-cost draw when discarded by Prepared or Survivor, keeping the engine running.
Granted crucial Dexterity and Thorns for free when discarded, enabling full blocks against heavy incoming damage.
Zero-cost enabler that consistently triggered Sly effects like Reflex++ and Abrasive.
Allowed the deck to hold Sly cards or discard enablers until the other half of the combo was drawn.
Provided heavy single-target poison scaling against elites like the Infested Prism.
Often held in hand or played for minimal impact compared to the poison engine, diluting the deck's focus.

coherence_analysis: Excellent synergy between discard enablers and Sly cards provided free draw, defense, and poison, though some attack cards like Memento Mori diluted the poison focus.

archetype: Strength scaling via self-damage and powers into multi-hit burst
build_summary: A highly synergistic self-damage and exhaust deck that rapidly scaled Strength to fuel massive multi-hit attacks against Vulnerable enemies.
primary_plan: Strength scaling via self-damage and powers into multi-hit burst
damage_engine: Strength (Rupture, Demon Form) + Multi-hit (Sword Boomerang, Whirlwind) + Vulnerable (Dominate, Molten Fist)
defense_engine: Exhaust (Feel No Pain) + Plating (Stone Armor) + Vulnerable mitigation (Colossus)
cycle_engine: Burst draw (Offering, Battle Trance, Expertise, Burning Pact)
energy_engine: Self-damage energy (Bloodletting, Offering) and Pyre
weak_points: The deck relies heavily on setting up multiple powers and taking self-damage, which could be risky against fast, high-damage enemies if defensive pieces aren't drawn early.

key_cards.insight: Enabled massive Strength scaling through the deck's numerous self-damage cards like Inferno and Bloodletting.
Delivered huge burst damage by multiplying the accumulated Strength across 4 hits.
Provided critical block during setup turns as the deck exhausted its many self-exhausting cards and targeted exhausts.
Jump-started turns with draw and energy while triggering self-damage synergies.
Provided consistent extra energy each turn to play the large hand sizes enabled by Runic Pyramid.
Synergized perfectly with the deck's Vulnerable application to halve incoming damage.
Basic strikes were actively exhausted by True Grit and Burning Pact to make room for better cards.

coherence_analysis: Highly synergistic deck where self-damage fuels Strength and energy, exhaust fuels Block, and Vulnerable fuels both damage multiplication and defense.

build_summary: A hybrid deck that relied on Noxious Fumes and Snecko Skull for passive scaling damage, while using a mix of block cards and attack mitigation to survive.
damage_engine: Poison application via Noxious Fumes and Snecko Skull, supplemented by multi-hit attacks
defense_engine: Basic block cards amplified by Shadowmeld++, combined with Weak from Neutralize++ and Strength reduction from Piercing Wail
cycle_engine: Hand refill via Expertise++ and Backflip
energy_engine: Turn 1 burst from Very Hot Cocoa, otherwise base energy
weak_points: The deck lacked sufficient block scaling or fast enough damage to race the boss's escalating attacks, and was cluttered with basic Strikes and disjointed attacks.

archetype: Forge scaling Sovereign Blade with Intangible stalling
build_summary: The deck stalled combats using Apparitions for Intangible while passively and actively Forging the Sovereign Blade to massive damage levels for a one-shot kill.
primary_plan: Forge scaling Sovereign Blade with Intangible stalling

cycle_engine: Spoils of Battle and Guiding Star+ for card draw
energy_engine: Refine Blade for next-turn energy
weak_points: The deck lacked sustained defense once its Ethereal Apparitions exhausted, leaving it vulnerable to high-HP enemies that survived the initial Sovereign Blade burst.

Enabled passive, energy-free scaling of the Sovereign Blade over long combats.
The sole significant damage source, relying on Retain and Forge to reach lethal thresholds.
Cycled the deck to find Apparitions while simultaneously advancing the Forge win condition.
Forged the blade and provided crucial next-turn energy to play expensive setups or multiple cards.
Acted as the ultimate multiplier for the Sovereign Blade, though it fell short against the Slimed Berserker's massive HP.

coherence_analysis: Highly synergistic Forge and Intangible setup, but the over-reliance on exhausting Apparitions created a hard turn limit that high-HP bosses could outlast.
